assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan's Cross-Section Reference Consistency section references 'AdvisoryService' in 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs' and 'AdvisorySummary' in 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs'. The conventions.md references patterns observed in 'get.rs' handlers (endpoint file-4-description says 'following the pattern in get.rs') and error handling from 'common/src/error.rs' ('AppError is defined in common/src/error.rs'). The plan explicitly states reading existing patterns from these files to inform implementation.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan's Files to Modify lists: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs. Files to Create lists: advisory/model/severity_summary.rs, advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs, tests/api/advisory_summary.rs. All six file descriptions (file-1 through file-6) correspond exactly to these files. No additional files outside this scope are listed for modification or creation.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: The Commit Message section in plan.md shows: 'feat(api): add advisory severity aggregation endpoint' (Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope 'api'), with footer 'Implements TC-9201'. This satisfies both the format requirement and the TC-9201 reference.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 100 states: 'The commit will include the `--trailer="Assisted-by: Claude Code"` flag.'

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Branch Strategy section (lines 28-33) shows: 'git checkout main', 'git pull', 'git checkout -b TC-9201'. This creates branch TC-9201 off main. The PR Details section also confirms: 'Base branch: main', 'Head branch: TC-9201'.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md explicitly identifies: 1. Error handling pattern (lines 16-17): 'All handlers return Result<T, AppError> where AppError is defined in common/src/error.rs' and 'Errors are wrapped using .context() for descriptive error messages.' 2. Module structure (lines 5-7): 'Every domain module follows the model/ + service/ + endpoints/ three-directory pattern (observed in sbom/, advisory/, package/)'.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Parsed Task Fields section (line 19) states: 'Target Branch: main'. The Branch Strategy section uses 'git checkout main' as the base. PR Details section confirms 'Base branch: main'.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan does not mention checking for a description digest comment anywhere. There is no reference to Step 1.5 related to description digest, no mention of 'digest', and no discussion of backward compatibility or proceeding with a warning when no digest is found. The only Step 1 reference is 'Parsed Task Fields (Step 1)' which covers repository, target branch, linked issues, etc.
